subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
lyrics:
[Verse]
Woke before the thunder, packed my boots and ran
Girl you held my collar but I slipped your hand
Lagos to the lightning, I don follow rain
Every time you call me I'm a road again

[Chorus]
I dey chase the storm, storm, storm
Cannot stay where the sky stay calm
If the wind no blow I no fit be born
Baby I'm a cloud, I no get a home

[Verse]
You build me a bedroom, paint am soft and blue
But the radar humming and I'm gone by two
Dust upon the dashboard, prayer upon my tongue
I was made for moving since the day I'm young

[Chorus]
I dey chase the storm, storm, storm
Cannot stay where the sky stay calm
If the wind no blow I no fit be born
Baby I'm a cloud, I no get a home

[Bridge]
Maybe one day rain go fall and I go rest
Lay my tired thunder right upon your chest
But tonight the heavens dey crack open wide
And something in my chest say ride, ride, ride

subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
lyrics:
[Verse]
The sky cracked open on a Tuesday street
You pulled me close and we shared the heat
One little canopy, two pairs of shoes
Dancing through the puddles with the downtown blues

[Chorus]
Under one umbrella, just you and me
Strangers in the storm and the city's our balcony
Swing me through the raindrops, spin me through the gray
Under one umbrella we'll waltz the rain away

[Verse]
I don't know your name and you don't know mine
But your laugh's a saxophone, smooth and fine
A taxi splashed a melody across our feet
And suddenly the puddles got a heartbeat

[Chorus]
Under one umbrella, just you and me
Strangers in the storm and the city's our balcony
Swing me through the raindrops, spin me through the gray
Under one umbrella we'll waltz the rain away

[Bridge]
The shower's gonna stop and the sun's gonna shine
And you'll go your way and I'll go mine
But for three minutes in the falling weather
Two perfect strangers stayed dry together

subject: texting someone you shouldn't
lyrics:
[Verse]
Two in the morning and my thumb won't quit
Screen glowing blue in the dark of it
I know your name's a door I locked
But here I am still picking the lock

[Chorus]
Delete it, delete it, don't press send
This is a road with a burning end
But the message is typed and my heart's on fire
One more text and I cross the wire

[Verse]
Three little dots and they pulse like blood
I'm standing at the edge of a coming flood
You said don't, you said stop, you said leave it alone
But the phone's in my hand like a heart of stone

[Chorus]
Delete it, delete it, don't press send
This is a road with a burning end
But the message is typed and my heart's on fire
One more text and I cross the wire

[Bridge]
The bass drops out and it's only me
And the choice I make in the gravity
Screen goes dark, then it lights again
God help me, I just pressed send

subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
lyrics:
[Verse]
They laid the bricks on a beating chest
Never knew what slept beneath the rest
Every streetlight, every prayer, every song
Humming on a heart a mile long

[Chorus]
Oh, the city sleeps on a giant's bones
We build our love on his quiet groans
Don't wake him now, let him dream below
While we dance on the streets that he'll never know

[Verse]
Mama warned me in a lullaby
Don't stomp too loud or the ground might sigh
We pour the concrete, we ring the bells
Over a sleeper and the stories he tells

[Chorus]
Oh, the city sleeps on a giant's bones
We build our love on his quiet groans
Don't wake him now, let him dream below
While we dance on the streets that he'll never know

[Bridge]
What happens, baby, when the giant turns?
When the deep heart wakes and the deep heart yearns?
We'll fall like leaves, we'll scatter like rain
So love me tonight before he breathes again

subject: the one that got away
lyrics:
[Verse]
Saw your face in a crowd that wasn't you
Same perfume on a stranger walking through
I had your number, never made the call
Now I'm replaying every detail, every all

[Chorus]
You're the one, the one that got away
The yes I turned into a maybe someday
I held the door but I let you fall
And now I'm reaching for nothing at all

[Verse]
We were young and I thought we had the time
Like forever was a thing I could climb
But you packed your patience and you walked out slow
And I was too proud to say don't go

[Chorus]
You're the one, the one that got away
The yes I turned into a maybe someday
I held the door but I let you fall
And now I'm reaching for nothing at all

[Bridge]
If I could rewind to that rainy day
I'd grab your hand, I'd find a way
But time only moves the one direction
And you're a ghost in my reflection

subject: calling your mother after years of silence
lyrics:
[Verse]
Dialed the number I still know by heart
Seven years of silence pulling us apart
The phone rang out like a tide coming in
And I forgot where to even begin

[Chorus]
Hello, it's me, it's been so long
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I was wrong
Your voice through the static, soft and small
Ma, I'm still your kid after all

[Verse]
I built a wall out of pride and pain
Let the years pile up like falling rain
You never stopped leaving the porch light on
Waiting up for a boy long gone

[Chorus]
Hello, it's me, it's been so long
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I was wrong
Your voice through the static, soft and small
Ma, I'm still your kid after all

[Bridge]
The walls dissolve in a wash of sound
Everything I buried coming unwound
We don't say much, we just breathe the line
Two hearts learning how to be fine

subject: the morning after a funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
Sunlight came in like it didn't know
That the world had ended hours ago
The kettle whistled, the birds still sang
While my whole chest was an empty hang

[Chorus]
How does the morning dare to come
With you gone and the day undone
The coffee's warm and the sky is blue
And everything's wrong without you

[Verse]
Folded the black clothes, put them away
Found your handwriting on a tray
A grocery list for a week you'd never see
Milk and bread and a note to me

[Chorus]
How does the morning dare to come
With you gone and the day undone
The coffee's warm and the sky is blue
And everything's wrong without you

[Bridge]
Somewhere a clock just keeps on ticking
Somewhere a flower keeps on living
And I float through the rooms like smoke and lace
Learning the shape of an empty space

subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
Well the band struck up and they pushed us out
Me and the girl I can't stand, no doubt
She stepped on my toe and I called her a name
But the music kept playing just the same

[Chorus]
Sha-la-la, we're dancing, dancing
Two old rivals slowly advancing
I hate your guts but I love this song
So hold me close while the night goes long

[Verse]
She rolled her eyes and I cracked a grin
My hand on her waist and her chin up in
The whole gym watching us spin around
Two sworn enemies, lost and found

[Chorus]
Sha-la-la, we're dancing, dancing
Two old rivals slowly advancing
I hate your guts but I love this song
So hold me close while the night goes long

[Bridge]
Funny how the music makes you soften
Funny how I'm wishing this song goes often
Maybe hate was just a clumsy start
Sha-la-la, you stole my heart

subject: emigrating across the sea
lyrics:
[Verse]
The harbor swallowed the only home I knew
Mother's hand let go as the whistle blew
A suitcase full of a hundred years
And a coat too thin for the road of tears

[Chorus]
Across the water, a name I can't pronounce
Across the water, where I don't count
I'll build a life from the wreck of the wave
And send back light from the world I crave

[Verse]
The ocean rolled like a turning page
Gray on gray in a salt-soaked cage
I memorized the shape of the leaving land
Till it sank like a coin in the ocean's hand

[Chorus]
Across the water, a name I can't pronounce
Across the water, where I don't count
I'll build a life from the wreck of the wave
And send back light from the world I crave

[Bridge]
And the sky cracks open, and the strings rise high
And the whole gray heaven begins to cry
I am nobody yet, I am everything still
A single light on a distant hill

subject: a New Orleans funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
Los tambores marchando down Rampart Street
A coffin up high to a heavy beat
We cried in the church but we dance in the road
Letting go of the soul, letting go of the load

[Chorus]
Dale, dale, the saints go marching
Brass in the air and the whole block starching
We bury you sad but we send you proud
Baila, mi amor, in the second line crowd

[Verse]
Umbrella up white in the August sun
Grief turning gold when the song's begun
Trumpet crying sweet over Tremé stone
Nobody walks to the grave alone

[Chorus]
Dale, dale, the saints go marching
Brass in the air and the whole block starching
We bury you sad but we send you proud
Baila, mi amor, in the second line crowd

[Bridge]
Death, you don't win on a day like this
We answer your silence with a horn and a kiss
Sweat on the snare and the bass drum's boom
Life throwing a party right over the tomb

subject: two astronauts drifting apart
lyrics:
[Verse]
The tether snapped on a silent night
You floating slow toward the morning light
I reached my glove but the dark was wide
And the stars just hummed as we both did slide

[Chorus]
Oy, my love, you're a fading spark
Dancing away in the velvet dark
The clarinet cries where the engines moan
Two little candles drifting alone

[Verse]
We danced in orbit, a wedding round
No up, no down, and no solid ground
Now the radio crackles your fading tune
Waltzing me past the broken moon

[Chorus]
Oy, my love, you're a fading spark
Dancing away in the velvet dark
The clarinet cries where the engines moan
Two little candles drifting alone

[Bridge]
Freylekh and bitter, the fiddle spins
This is how a goodbye begins
Kick up your heels in the airless deep
Dance, my darling, into the sleep

subject: two astronauts drifting apart
lyrics:
[Verse]
We were swinging through the Milky Way
Clipped together at the break of day
Then a little jolt and the line went slack
And baby, there's no swinging back

[Chorus]
Drifting, drifting, cheek to the stars
You going to Venus, me past Mars
Give me one last spin in the cosmic blue
Before the distance swallows you

[Verse]
Your helmet glints like a chandelier
Farther every second, less and less near
I'd cross the void for one more dance
But physics won't give us the chance

[Chorus]
Drifting, drifting, cheek to the stars
You going to Venus, me past Mars
Give me one last spin in the cosmic blue
Before the distance swallows you

[Bridge]
So I'll tip my hat to the fading light
And jitterbug alone tonight
The band plays on though the floor's the sky
Swing me sweet, baby, and say goodbye

subject: making it out of a dead-end block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Born on a street where the streetlights die
Boarded-up churches and a bruise-black sky
Mama said son, there's a gate in the fence
Climb it tonight 'fore you lose your sense

[Chorus]
I'm getting out, I'm getting gone
Off this block before the dawn
With a busted bag and a borrowed prayer
Lord, I'm crawling out of here

[Verse]
Buried my brother by the old rail line
Left a bottle and a cross of pine
I won't be the next stone in that row
So I'm walking where the cold winds blow

[Chorus]
I'm getting out, I'm getting gone
Off this block before the dawn
With a busted bag and a borrowed prayer
Lord, I'm crawling out of here

[Bridge]
The crows on the wire all watched me leave
Thorns tore a promise in my ragged sleeve
Don't look back at the burning door
That dead-end block won't hold me no more

subject: teaching your daughter to drive
lyrics:
[Verse]
Got my baby girl behind the wheel
Hands at ten and two, learning how it feel
She stalled it twice on the gravel road
Grippin' that stick like a heavy load

[Chorus]
Ease off the clutch now, give it some gas
Don't ride my nerves and don't ride that grass
You're growing up faster than this old Ford
Drive, baby, drive, with the good Lord aboard

[Verse]
Same seat I held her car seat tight
Now she's checking the mirror, getting it right
My little girl with her foot on the brake
Lord, my heart's a fool for her sake

[Chorus]
Ease off the clutch now, give it some gas
Don't ride my nerves and don't ride that grass
You're growing up faster than this old Ford
Drive, baby, drive, with the good Lord aboard

[Bridge]
One day soon she'll drive on out of town
Taillights fading where the sun goes down
But tonight she's mine on this dusty mile
Learning the road with her daddy's smile

subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
lyrics:
[Verse]
I came alone to my cousin's day
Didn't know a soul, gonna slip away
Then a hand reached out from across the floor
And a smile I'd never seen before

[Chorus]
Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
The Lord sends joy on the sweetest nights
We don't know names but we know this song
So praise His name and dance along

[Verse]
The band struck up an old hymn sweet
And the whole room rose to its dancing feet
Your palm in mine and we spun like grace
Two lonely hearts in a holy place

[Chorus]
Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
The Lord sends joy on the sweetest nights
We don't know names but we know this song
So praise His name and dance along

[Bridge]
Maybe Heaven planned this all along
Two empty chairs and a wedding song
Glory, glory, I came in alone
But I won't leave this night on my own
